context: Setting max_connections on a standby server relative to the master.
evidence_span: When running a standby server, you must set this parameter to the same or higher value than on the master server.

context: The value of superuser_reserved_connections must be less than max_connections.
evidence_span: The value must be less than max_connections.

context: max_prepared_transactions should be at least as large as max_connections for every session to have a prepared transaction pending.
evidence_span: If you are using prepared transactions, you will probably want max_prepared_transactions to be at least as large as max_connections , so that every session can have a prepared transaction pending.

context: On a standby server, max_prepared_transactions must be set to the same or higher value than on the master server.
evidence_span: When running a standby server, you must set this parameter to the same or higher value than on the master server.

context: standby server must set max_prepared_transactions to same or higher value than on master
evidence_span: you must set this parameter to the same or higher value than on the master server

context: hash_mem_multiplier multiplies work_mem to compute memory limit for hash operations.
evidence_span: The final limit is determined by multiplying work_mem by hash_mem_multiplier.

context: It's safe to set maintenance_work_mem larger than work_mem.
evidence_span: it's safe to set this value significantly larger than work_mem

context: Autovacuum may allocate up to autovacuum_max_workers times maintenance_work_mem, so autovacuum_work_mem can be set separately to control this.
evidence_span: It may be useful to control for this by separately setting autovacuum_work_mem

context: Default behavior when autovacuum_work_mem is -1
evidence_span: It defaults to -1, indicating that the value of maintenance_work_mem should be used instead.

context: Standby must have same or higher value than master.
evidence_span: When running a standby server, you must set this parameter to the same or higher value than on the master server.

context: Adjust max_parallel_workers when changing max_worker_processes.
evidence_span: When changing this value, consider also adjusting max_parallel_workers

context: Adjust max_parallel_maintenance_workers when changing max_worker_processes.
evidence_span: When changing this value, consider also adjusting max_parallel_maintenance_workers

context: Adjust max_parallel_workers_per_gather when changing max_worker_processes.
evidence_span: When changing this value, consider also adjusting max_parallel_workers_per_gather

context: Standby server must have same or higher value than master.
evidence_span: When running a standby server, you must set this parameter to the same or higher value than on the master server.

context: Adjust related parallel worker knobs when changing max_worker_processes.
evidence_span: When changing this value, consider also adjusting max_parallel_workers , max_parallel_maintenance_workers , and max_parallel_workers_per_gather .

context: Adjust related parallel worker knobs when changing max_worker_processes.
evidence_span: When changing this value, consider also adjusting max_parallel_workers , max_parallel_maintenance_workers , and max_parallel_workers_per_gather .

context: Adjust related parallel worker knobs when changing max_worker_processes.
evidence_span: When changing this value, consider also adjusting max_parallel_workers , max_parallel_maintenance_workers , and max_parallel_workers_per_gather .

context: Parallel workers are limited by max_parallel_workers.
evidence_span: Parallel workers are taken from the pool of processes established by max_worker_processes , limited by max_parallel_workers .

context: Resource limits like work_mem apply per worker, affecting total resource utilization.
evidence_span: This should be taken into account when choosing a value for this setting, as well as when configuring other settings that control resource utilization, such as work_mem .

context: Parallel workers are limited by max_parallel_workers.
evidence_span: Parallel workers are taken from the pool of processes established by max_worker_processes , limited by max_parallel_workers .

context: Parallel workers are taken from the pool of processes established by max_worker_processes.
evidence_span: Parallel workers are taken from the pool of processes established by max_worker_processes , limited by max_parallel_workers .

context: Parallel utility commands treat maintenance_work_mem as a limit for the entire command.
evidence_span: Parallel utility commands treat the resource limit maintenance_work_mem as a limit to be applied to the entire utility command, regardless of the number of parallel worker processes.

context: Adjust related knobs when changing max_parallel_workers
evidence_span: When increasing or decreasing this value, consider also adjusting max_parallel_maintenance_workers and max_parallel_workers_per_gather .

context: Adjust related knobs when changing max_parallel_workers
evidence_span: When increasing or decreasing this value, consider also adjusting max_parallel_maintenance_workers and max_parallel_workers_per_gather .

relation: less than or equal to half
context: max_parallel_workers limited by max_worker_processes
evidence_span: a setting for this value which is higher than max_worker_processes will have no effect, since parallel workers are taken from the pool of worker processes established by that setting.

context: WAL archiving and streaming replication require wal_level set to replica or higher.
evidence_span: replica or higher must be used to enable WAL archiving ( archive_mode ) and streaming replication.

context: Turning off fsync suggests turning off full_page_writes.
evidence_span: If you turn this parameter off, also consider turning off full_page_writes .

context: synchronous_commit controls waiting for standby WAL processing when synchronous_standby_names is set
evidence_span: If synchronous_standby_names is non-empty, synchronous_commit also controls whether transaction commits will wait for their WAL records to be processed on the standby server(s).

context: Maximum delay in off mode is three times wal_writer_delay
evidence_span: The maximum delay is three times wal_writer_delay.

context: Non-off modes require fsync for local WAL flush.
evidence_span: The local behavior of all non- off modes is to wait for local flush of WAL to disk.

context: Maximum delay in off mode is three times wal_writer_delay.
evidence_span: The maximum delay is three times wal_writer_delay .

condition: when synchronous replication is in use
context: synchronous_commit settings require synchronous_standby_names to be enabled for replication effects
evidence_span: This is usually not desirable when synchronous replication is in use

context: Default size is 1/32nd of shared_buffers.
evidence_span: The default setting of -1 selects a size equal to 1/32nd (about 3%) of shared_buffers

context: Default size not less than 64kB nor more than one WAL segment.
evidence_span: but not less than 64kB nor more than the size of one WAL segment, typically 16MB

condition: at least commit_siblings other transactions are active
context: Delay only performed if a minimum number of sibling transactions are active.
evidence_span: a delay is only performed if at least commit_siblings other transactions are active when a flush is about to be initiated.

context: No delays are performed if fsync is disabled.
evidence_span: no delays are performed if fsync is disabled.

context: Checkpoint warning requires checkpoint_timeout to be at least as large as checkpoint_warning to generate warnings.
evidence_span: No warnings will be generated if checkpoint_timeout is less than checkpoint_warning.

context: archive_mode cannot be enabled when wal_level is minimal
evidence_span: archive_mode cannot be enabled when wal_level is set to minimal .

context: archive_command is ignored unless archive_mode is enabled at server start
evidence_span: It is ignored unless archive_mode was enabled at server start.

context: archive_timeout forces WAL segment switches to limit unarchived data age
evidence_span: To limit how old unarchived data can be, you can set archive_timeout to force the server to switch to a new WAL segment file periodically.

context: recovery_target_inclusive applies when recovery_target_lsn is specified
evidence_span: Applies when recovery_target_lsn , recovery_target_time , or recovery_target_xid is specified.

context: recovery_target_inclusive applies when recovery_target_time is specified
evidence_span: Applies when recovery_target_lsn , recovery_target_time , or recovery_target_xid is specified.

context: recovery_target_inclusive applies when recovery_target_xid is specified
evidence_span: Applies when recovery_target_lsn , recovery_target_time , or recovery_target_xid is specified.

context: max_wal_senders requires wal_level to be replica or higher for standby connections.
evidence_span: wal_level must be set to replica or higher to allow connections from standby servers.

context: On a standby server, max_wal_senders must be same or higher than on the master.
evidence_span: When running a standby server, you must set this parameter to the same or higher value than on the master server.

context: max_replication_slots requires wal_level to be replica or higher for replication slots to be used.
evidence_span: wal_level must be set to replica or higher to allow replication slots to be used.

context: Alternative to using vacuum_defer_cleanup_age
evidence_span: You should also consider setting hot_standby_feedback on standby server(s) as an alternative to using this parameter.

context: Does not prevent cleanup of dead rows which have reached the age specified by old_snapshot_threshold
evidence_span: This does not prevent cleanup of dead rows which have reached the age specified by old_snapshot_threshold .

context: enable_indexonlyscan requires enable_indexscan to be enabled for the planner to consider index-only-scans.
evidence_span: The enable_indexscan setting must also be enabled to have the query planner consider index-only-scans.

context: Setting random_page_cost less than seq_page_cost is not physically sensible.
evidence_span: Although the system will let you set random_page_cost to less than seq_page_cost , it is not physically sensible to do so.

condition: if the database is entirely cached in RAM
context: Setting random_page_cost equal to seq_page_cost makes sense if the database is entirely cached in RAM.
evidence_span: However, setting them equal makes sense if the database is entirely cached in RAM, since in that case there is no penalty for touching pages out of sequence.

context: jit_inline_above_cost should not be set less than jit_above_cost
evidence_span: It is not meaningful to set this to less than jit_above_cost .

context: jit_optimize_above_cost should not be set less than jit_above_cost
evidence_span: It is not meaningful to set this to less than jit_above_cost

relation: less than or equal to half
context: jit_optimize_above_cost unlikely beneficial if set more than jit_inline_above_cost
evidence_span: it is unlikely to be beneficial to set it to more than jit_inline_above_cost

condition: If it is set to zero (the default setting)
context: Default value for geqo_generations is chosen based on geqo_pool_size.
evidence_span: If it is set to zero (the default setting) then a suitable value is chosen based on geqo_pool_size.

condition: Setting this value to geqo_threshold or more may trigger use of the GEQO planner
context: Setting from_collapse_limit too high may trigger GEQO planner
evidence_span: Setting this value to geqo_threshold or more may trigger use of the GEQO planner, resulting in non-optimal plans.

context: Default setting for join_collapse_limit
evidence_span: By default, this variable is set the same as from_collapse_limit

condition: when join_collapse_limit is set to geqo_threshold or more
context: Triggering GEQO planner
evidence_span: Setting this value to geqo_threshold or more may trigger use of the GEQO planner

condition: when set to geqo_threshold or more
context: Setting join_collapse_limit may trigger GEQO planner
evidence_span: Setting this value to geqo_threshold or more may trigger use of the GEQO planner

context: Sets permissions for log files when logging_collector is enabled.
evidence_span: On Unix systems this parameter sets the permissions for log files when logging_collector is enabled.

condition: to make use of such a setting
context: Altering log_directory is needed for certain log_file_mode settings.
evidence_span: Note however that to make use of such a setting, you'll need to alter log_directory to store the files somewhere outside the cluster data directory.

context: log_file_mode sets permissions when logging_collector is enabled
evidence_span: On Unix systems this parameter sets the permissions for log files when logging_collector is enabled.

context: to use group-readable permissions, log_directory must be outside cluster data directory
evidence_span: Note however that to make use of such a setting, you'll need to alter log_directory to store the files somewhere outside the cluster data directory.

context: log_truncate_on_rotation works with log_filename for automatic log overwriting
evidence_span: set log_filename to server_log.%a , log_truncate_on_rotation to on

context: log_truncate_on_rotation works with log_rotation_age for time-based log rotation and overwriting
evidence_span: log_truncate_on_rotation to on , and log_rotation_age to 1440

context: log_truncate_on_rotation works with log_rotation_size for size-driven log rotation and overwriting
evidence_span: log_truncate_on_rotation to on , log_rotation_age to 60 , and log_rotation_size to 1000000

context: log_min_duration_sample has lower priority than log_min_duration_statement
evidence_span: This setting has lower priority than log_min_duration_statement , meaning that statements with durations exceeding log_min_duration_statement are not subject to sampling and are always logged.

context: sample rate controlled by log_statement_sample_rate
evidence_span: only for a subset of the executed statements, with sample rate controlled by log_statement_sample_rate

condition: log_duration is on and log_min_duration_statement has a positive value
context: Interaction between logging duration and query text based on threshold
evidence_span: if log_duration is on and log_min_duration_statement has a positive value, all durations are logged but the query text is included only for statements exceeding the threshold

context: autovacuum requires track_counts to be enabled to work.
evidence_span: track_counts must also be enabled for autovacuum to work.

context: lock_timeout should be less than statement_timeout to avoid being overridden
evidence_span: if statement_timeout is nonzero, it is rather pointless to set lock_timeout to the same or larger value, since the statement timeout would always trigger first.

context: log_min_error_statement must be set appropriately to log lock_timeout statements
evidence_span: If log_min_error_statement is set to ERROR or lower, the statement that timed out will be logged.

relation: less than or equal to half
context: VACUUM limits effective value to 95% of autovacuum_multixact_freeze_max_age.
evidence_span: VACUUM will silently limit the effective value to 95% of autovacuum_multixact_freeze_max_age

relation: less than or equal to half
context: VACUUM limits effective value to half of autovacuum_multixact_freeze_max_age to avoid short forced autovacuum intervals.
evidence_span: VACUUM will silently limit the effective value to half the value of autovacuum_multixact_freeze_max_age

context: Lock table size calculation
evidence_span: max_locks_per_transaction * ( max_connections + max_prepared_transactions )

context: Lock table size calculation
evidence_span: max_locks_per_transaction * ( max_connections + max_prepared_transactions )

context: Standby server requirement
evidence_span: When running a standby server, you must set this parameter to the same or higher value than on the master server.

context: Maximum segment file size calculation
evidence_span: The maximum size of a segment file in bytes is equal to segment_size multiplied by block_size

context: trace_notify output to client requires client_min_messages at DEBUG1 or lower
evidence_span: client_min_messages or log_min_messages must be DEBUG1 or lower to send this output to the client or server logs, respectively.

context: trace_notify output to server logs requires log_min_messages at DEBUG1 or lower
evidence_span: client_min_messages or log_min_messages must be DEBUG1 or lower to send this output to the client or server logs, respectively.
